creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
Created attachment 247896
context and log output

I'm running into random strange connection problems. The wifi router
is only some 10 to 12 feet away. The problem only occurs during
boot. Worse, attempting to restart a connection process
(service netif restart) more often than not, results in an immediate
reboot. I encountered this while on releng/14. It appears to me to be
related to i915kms. As both problems only occur when that module is
loaded. I'm now on 15, as Alder lake is only supported on CURRENT.

I'm pasting the relevant info below. But am also adding it as an attachment
for easier reading. I can provide more info as needed/requested.

Thanks.

--Chris

Jan 21 23:20:53 fbsd15 kernel: CPU: 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i3-1215U (2496.00-MHz K8-class CPU)

    device     = 'Alder Lake-P PCH CNVi WiFi'

    device     = 'Alder Lake-UP3 GT1 [UHD Graphics]'
    class      = display
    subclass   = VGA
